commit.message:
Fix bulk email by sending one-at-a-time instead of single large payload

The GET URL was exceeding length limits when the full email body + all rows
were encoded into one URL parameter. Now bulk sends loop on the client,
calling sendCustomEmail per recipient with resolveTags flag for merge tags.
Progress counter updates live as each email sends.

commit.message:
fix: fix regex word boundary bug preventing physics question routing to TikZ

The regex \baccelerates?\b was written as \baccelerates\b which requires
a word boundary after 't' — but "accelerates" has 'e' after 't', so no
boundary exists. Changed to explicit word forms (acceleration|accelerates).
Also added broader F=ma and force+kg/mass patterns to catch physics questions.

commit.message:
fix: add RFC 9728 protected resource metadata endpoint

Gemini CLI and newer MCP SDK clients hit /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource
before /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server. Without it, OAuth discovery
fails with 'Failed to discover OAuth configuration'.

Returns: { resource: '/mcp', authorization_servers: [base] }

commit.message:
feat: CPI-adjusted floating ratio for JUL pricing oracle

JUL backed by fixed PoW work, but API token value changes over time.
Fixed ratio creates arbitrage. Solution: float the ratio.

ratio = baseRatio × (referenceCost / currentRealCost)
realCost = nominalCost × (referenceCPI / currentCPI)

If API gets cheaper → ratio up (more tokens, same real value).
If dollar inflates → ratio up (compensates for debasement).
Phase 3: AMM price discovery replaces manual oracle.

compute-economics.js: Pricing oracle state (costPerMTok, cpiIndex),
getJulToPoolRatio(), updatePricing(), getPricingInfo().
mining.js: julToTokens() replaces fixed JUL_PER_API_TOKEN constant.
index.js: /reprice owner command, ratio shown in /balance + /economy.
Article updated: floating ratio, no-arbitrage explanation.

fix(hooks): replace dangerous zsh -ic version detection with file content extraction

The interactive shell approach (`zsh -ic 'claude --version'`) caused CC to
crash: npx tried TTY output from a background subprocess, zsh suspended the
entire process tree. Replace with safe file content extraction that reads
the version from the claude binary without executing it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

GPU perf optimizations: eliminate trig, branches, and per-frame allocs

- Particle spin: cos/sin → small-angle approximation (saves 8M trig ops/frame)
- Prismatic color: 3 cos → 2 via addition identity (saves 4M trig ops/frame)
- grainTint: 5 if-branches → branchless step/mix chain (eliminates warp divergence)
- Display shader: pow(spec,8) and pow(spec,2) → repeated squaring (3 muls)
- Curl noise hash: sin-based → integer hash (eliminates sin from noise octaves)
- Hoist bloom Float32Array to module scope (eliminates per-frame GC pressure)
- Skip splatCountBuf write when no splats active

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve all pending pipeline issues — CNAES download, 404 handling, clock drift, pagination, post-ingest automation

- reference_tasks: auto-download RF misc ZIPs (CNAEs, Naturezas, Qualificacoes, Municipios, Paises, Motivos) before seeding so CNAES.csv absence no longer silently skips RF reference data
- senado: handle HTTP 404/204 gracefully in _fetch_ceaps and _get_senator_codes — return ([], next_cursor) with warning log instead of raising; fixes senator 5672 year 2022 upstream gap
- portal_transparencia: fix pagination stopping at page 1 — change len(records) >= DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE(100) to len(records) > 0 in all three fetch paths (simple, windowed, dimension-windowed); PT API returns 10-20 items/page not 100
- portal_transparencia: extend default window from -59 to -60 months (exactly 5 years) in all 6 occurrences
- tse: expand _ELECTION_YEARS from [2024] to [2024, 2022, 2020] to cover 5-year window
- compras_gov, comprasnet_contratos, querido_diario: treat HTTP 404 as no-data ([], None) with warning log instead of raising
- docker-compose: add cap_add SYS_TIME and /etc/localtime:ro mount to all 6 worker services to fix ~25s clock drift
- worker/Dockerfile: install ntpdate; add docker-entrypoint.sh that syncs time at startup (non-fatal)
- ingest_tasks: auto-dispatch trigger_post_ingest_recompute (countdown=120s) after full ingest of portal_transparencia/senado/camara/pncp
- maintenance_tasks: add trigger_post_ingest_recompute task that chains compute_all_baselines + run_all_signals
- tests: 159 passing, 0 failures

Show 10% preview layout while full compute runs in background

When no cached layout exists, compute a deterministic 10% subsample
(~7.5K molecules, ~1 min) for instant visual feedback, then run the
full 75K-molecule compute in a background thread. A polling fragment
checks S3 every 10s and auto-refreshes when the full layout is ready.
This avoids the blank-page timeout that killed every prior session.

The game requires ships to travel to a jump_gate POI before they can
jump to another system. The previous code assumed direct jumping was
possible.

- Undock if docked before traveling to jump gate
- Travel to jump gate if not already there
- Then execute jump to target system

fix(approval): include PolicySurface in fingerprint canonical string

Add the policy surface to the canonical string used for approval
fingerprinting. Without this, an approval token issued for a Robot-
surface action could be consumed by an Mcp-surface action with the
same action kind and pane, bypassing surface-scoped authorization.

- Add "surface={surface}|" to fingerprint_for_input canonical string
- Update existing test to pin surface so actor-kind independence still
  holds when surface is fixed
- Add fingerprint_surface_changes_hash test verifying different
  surfaces produce different fingerprints
- Add approval_different_policy_surface_prevents_consumption
  integration test confirming token scoping
- Add PolicySurface to proptest strategy (arb_policy_surface) and
  wire into arb_policy_input for property-based coverage

commit.message:
fix: GAM 광고 빌드 수정 + 소모임 전체 목록 페이지 (#240)

## Summary
- CI에서 `.env` 파일 명시적 생성으로 GAM 광고 코드(`23338387889`) 번들 포함 보장
- 빌드 후 GAM 네트워크 코드 검증 단계 추가 (누락 시 빌드 실패 처리)
- `/groups` 소모임 전체 목록 SSR 페이지 신규 추가 (DB 캐시 10분, Redis 30분)
- 로그인 페이지 리팩토링

## 변경 사항
| 파일 | 내용 |
|------|------|
| `.github/workflows/deploy-binary.yml` | VITE_ .env 파일 생성 + GAM 검증 |
| `apps/web/src/routes/groups/+page.server.ts` | 소모임 목록 SSR 로드 (신규) |
| `apps/web/src/routes/groups/+page.svelte` | 소모임 목록 UI (신규) |
| `apps/web/src/routes/login/+page.svelte` | 로그인 페이지 리팩토링 |

## 안전성
- 기존 라우트/기능 변경 없음 (새 라우트 추가만)
- GAM 수정은 CI 워크플로우 변경이라 런타임 영향 없음
- DB 메뉴 URL은 이미 `/groups`로 업데이트 완료

## Test plan
- [ ] `/groups` 페이지 접속 → 소모임 목록 표시 확인
- [ ] 소모임 검색 동작 확인
- [ ] 사이드바 메뉴에서 "소모임 모아보기" 클릭 시 `/groups` 이동 확인
- [ ] 배포 후 GAM 광고 코드 번들 포함 검증 (`23338387889`)
- [ ] 기존 게시판, 수익링크, 로그인 정상 동작 확인

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Deploy Bot <deploy@damoang.net>

commit.message:
Fix alternative discovery: stop writing to GENERATED total_score column

The dynamic swap discovery engine (findDynamicSwaps) was silently failing
because saveDiscoveries tried to INSERT into total_score, which is a
GENERATED ALWAYS column in PostgreSQL. Every INSERT threw an error caught
by the per-candidate try/catch, so the function always returned [].

Two fixes:
- Replace total_score INSERT with the three component scores
  (nutrition_score, additives_score, organic_bonus) that feed the
  generated column
- Add missing swap_discovery_type and swap_discovered_at columns
  to the DB migration in init.js (referenced but never created)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(calendar): replace DEBUG_GRID URL param with process.env check (#18)

DEBUG_GRID now uses process.env.NODE_ENV + REACT_APP_DEBUG_GRID
instead of URL param. No debug code runs in production builds.

feat(ingest,ipc): integrate PaneArena allocator tracking and expose allocator stats in IPC status

ingest.rs:
- Add PaneArena field to PaneEntry, tracking a per-pane logical
  allocator arena reservation for memory isolation between panes
- Reserve arenas on pane discovery via PaneArenaRegistry, release
  on pane removal, ensuring arena lifecycle matches pane lifecycle
- Add pane_arena(), pane_arena_count(), and pane_arenas_snapshot()
  accessors on PaneRegistry for external introspection
- Update all PaneEntry::new/new_with_uuid call sites to pass arena

ipc.rs:
- Add allocator_status_payload() helper that surfaces the allocator
  backend name (system/jemalloc), jemalloc enabled flag, and memory
  stats (allocated, active, resident, mapped, retained) or error
- Include allocator status and pane arena inventory in the IPC
  status endpoint response, enabling operators to monitor per-pane
  memory isolation health via the wa.status command

Cargo.toml:
- Add frankenterm-alloc workspace dependency to frankenterm-core

Fix config not surviving reboot: atomic writes, never redirect to /tmp

Root cause: save logic was setting config_path = '/tmp/tidbyt_config.json'
when the primary write failed, causing all subsequent saves to land in /tmp
which is wiped on every reboot.

Fixes:
- Add _write_config_to_disk(): writes to <path>.tmp then os.replace() so a
  failed write never corrupts or deletes the existing config file
- config_path is never reassigned; /tmp is gone as a save destination
- load_config() now tries /tmp as a read-only fallback and migrates the
  config back to the primary path if found there (handles existing installs)
- _setup_default_config() and save_config() both use the new method
- save_raw_config in web.py delegates to _write_config_to_disk() instead
  of duplicating the broken logic; shows a clear error if save fails

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
